task_completion.description: Ardour is open and the target track is created with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ardour interface is visible, and a track header shows the name 'Vinyl Pre-Master'.

task_completion.description: The required source audio files are imported into the Ardour session.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Audio regions corresponding to 'moonlight_sonata' and 'narration' are visible in the editor timeline.

task_completion.description: Both audio files are sequenced on a single track.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The two audio regions are placed on the same horizontal track lane ('Vinyl Pre-Master') in the Ardour editor window.

task_completion.description: A gap is left between the two sequenced audio files.
task_completion.visual_evidence: There is a visible empty space on the timeline between the end of the first audio region and the start of the second audio region.

task_completion.description: A fade is applied to the audio sequence.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible fade curve (usually a diagonal line or shaded curve at the top corner of an audio region) is present on the timeline.

task_completion.description: The sequence is exported to the correct delivery directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An Ardour export dialog box shows the destination path '/home/ga/Audio/vinyl_delivery/' (and ideally 44.1 kHz sample rate), or a file manager shows the exported WAV file in that specific directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Ardour GUI to sequence the audio.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Ardour application interface being actively interacted with (e.g., mouse cursor moving regions, active windows), rather than just terminal commands using SoX or FFmpeg.

integrity.description: The agent imported actual audio files rather than creating empty dummy regions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The audio regions on the Ardour timeline display visible, complex waveforms inside the blocks.

integrity.description: The agent used Ardour's export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Ardour 'Export' dialog or export progress bar is visible in at least one screenshot, proving the file was generated by the DAW.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target track in Ardour must be named 'Vinyl Pre-Master'. The sequence requires a 3.0-second gap between the two audio files and a 2.0-second fade. The final export must be directed to '/home/ga/Audio/vinyl_delivery' with a sample rate of 44100Hz (44.1 kHz).
